text:
Four-timeOlympicgold medal winner Matthew Pinsent has blasted the process that downgraded theTeam GBquartet of Alex Yee, Georgia Taylor-Brown, Sam Dickinson and Beth Potter from silver to bronze in the Olympic triathlon mixed relay on Monday morning.

The Brits played their part in a three-way sprint finish to decide the medal-paying positions with Potter crossing the line level with USA athlete Taylor Knibb. The pair were both trailing behind Laura Lindemann, who clinched gold for Germany in a thrilling spectacle on the Seine.

After a photo-finish analysis, officials judged that Potter had pipped her opponent to second place, awarding Team GB the silver medal and condemning the USA to bronze. However, upon a review later in the day, the decision was reversed with Knibb able to celebrate an improved classification with her team-mates.

The saga did not go down well with Pinsent, who took to the X social media platform to complain: “Total balls up by Tri officials to not get the photo finish absolutely right in the first instance. GB bronze correct but there are procedures in loads of sports to learn from that are better than this.

“As an addition to this - the finish ribbon/tape in tri is necessary and there just for the still photos. In certain circumstances it could block/impede the proper use of PF cameras. Needs to go.”

The decision was a brutal one for Team GB, who looked to be in control of the race during the final leg before cycling specialist Knibb hunted down Potter ahead of the final transition. The Brits won gold in this event in Tokyo but will have to settle for bronze in Paris.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Discussing the process, Team GB performance director Mike Cavendish explained: “They want to be able to put something up as soon as they can, and they thought it looked clear, but when you get the photo finish from both sides, it doesn’t look the same way.

“To be fair to World Triathlon, they went and got me the footage straight away, and as soon as you zoom in you can see it’s very, very tight but it is clear.”

Cavendish was then asked about the response of the Team GB athletes to the news. “Yeah, of course they’re disappointed, they would like to win a silver, not a bronze,” he explained.

“But I think that passes pretty quickly when they realise ultimately it’s an Olympic medal. An unbelievable race, it’s a fantastic advert for our sport, and that disappointment dissipates pretty quickly.”

title: Olympics legend left fuming after Team GB silver medal changed to bron | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Harry Smith
description: Team GB were dealt a brutal blow on Monday, losing out on a silver medal in agonising circumstances.
